title: File Handling

questions.objective_ref: Explain the difference between text files and binary files.
Explain the difference between absolute and relative paths.
Open a file with open() using the correct mode; 'w' truncates.
Open a file with open() using the correct mode; 'a' appends.
Read file contents with read(), readline(), and readlines().
Read file contents by looping over a file object (memory-friendly).
Write content with write() (does not add a newline).
Use the with statement (a context manager) so files are closed automatically.
Read tabular data from a CSV file using the csv module (newline='').
Read tabular data from a CSV file; values arrive as strings (rows as list of strings).
Load JSON into Python objects with json.load(); JSON to Python type mapping.
Use the with statement (a context manager) so files are closed automatically.

objectives_covered: text vs binary files; absolute vs relative paths
open() and modes (r, w, a, r+) — 'w' truncates, 'a' appends
read()/readline()/readlines() + looping over a file; write() (no auto newline)
csv module reading (newline='', rows as list of strings, DictReader)
json load/dump (files) vs loads/dumps (strings); JSON to Python type mapping
forward_reference_check: No try/except or exception-handling (7.2/7.3) content quizzed.
